question: Should Dennett accept the breaking pre-production M01 protocol epoch defined by ADR-006?
context: WP-M01-002 replaces the unused M00 dennett.v1 scaffold with the provider-neutral M01 System, Project and Session contracts. The change breaks only generated pre-production APIs, migrates no user data, passed the full local and GitHub gates, and passed detached R3 closure review.

options.description: Reject the M01 epoch and restore the M00 scaffold
benefits: avoids the intentional compile-time break of the M00 generated API
costs: blocks desktop and runtime implementation on the canonical subsystem contracts
restores the protocol and lint debt retired by this package
risks: a production client may begin depending on the known-placeholder protocol shape

options.description: Accept ADR-006 and merge the bounded M01 protocol epoch
benefits: gives Rust and TypeScript clients one typed provider-neutral contract
fences stale watch streams by Authority Epoch and separates admission from completion
retires the pre-production protocol and lint debt before production consumers exist
costs: all unused M00 generated imports and RPC names stop compiling
risks: later corrections must follow ordinary additive compatibility or a new explicit owner-gated epoch

reason: The epoch is bounded to M01 desktop conversation, carries no persisted-data migration, passed exact descriptor and compatibility checks, and closed every detached R3 finding.

resolution.note: Owner accepted ADR-006 and authorized the WP-M01-002 merge after reviewing a plain-language summary of the breaking pre-production epoch and its evidence.
